description: Minimal CMake C++ project (CMakeLists.txt + src/main.cpp), default CLion C/C++ profile. CLion auto-detects CMake and resolves the model itself, then flags the planted use-after-move + dead-store. The image must carry the CLion dist's native deps (QD-15107): the .NET ReSharper backend's runtime libs (ICU/SSL/krb5) or it SIGABRTs at startup, and the JBR's libfreetype for the CMake console editor — either miss wedges the headless analyzer at 'Awaits CLion backend activities' (it HANGS instead of failing). No log gate on 'Problems found loading plugins:' — the CLion dist's bundled Angular/HtmlTools warning is benign and the source qodana-cli smoke test omits the same check. Guards (QD-14974): the analyzer flags the planted use-after-move (under CLion's clang-tidy umbrella id 'ClangTidy', not a per-check id) and no 'unknown target triple' diagnostic poisons the TU. The planted dead store is also present in main.cpp but is NOT asserted — CLion's own DFA ('CppDFAUnusedValue') reports it non-deterministically across runs, whereas the clang-tidy use-after-move is stable. qodana-cpp (CLion/QDCPP, umbrella 'ClangTidy' + 'Cpp*' inspection ids) is distinct from qodana-clang (QDCLC, which emits per-check clang-tidy ids); the per-check FP guard (QD-14272) lives in the qodana-clang fixture, not here.

expectations.reason: The planted use-after-move. CLion runs clang-tidy and reports EVERY clang-tidy finding under the single umbrella inspection id 'ClangTidy' (NOT per-check ids like qodana-clang's 'bugprone-use-after-move'), so pin by ruleId=ClangTidy + the message. A silent zero means the analyzer found nothing (QD-14974). CI-pinned from run 27879871440.

A baked-in wrong target triple surfaces as an 'unknown target triple' diagnostic that poisons the whole TU (QD-14974). Asserted by message across ANY rule id (CLion does not surface it under a stable 'clang-diagnostic-error' id), so the two positive findings above could not be produced if the TU were poisoned. Must be absent.
